tasks.description: Establish Helixa API access — reach out, get API key, add to .env, smoke test endpoints
tasks.context:
Helixa (https://github.com/Bendr-20/helixa) is an onchain trust/reputation layer for AI agents on Base. API base: https://api.helixa.xyz. Auth: SIWA (Sign-In With Ethereum) — likely Bearer token after wallet auth. We need an API key to use authenticated endpoints.

STEPS (CTO-agent executes via Telegram/web):

STEP 1 — Smoke test unauthenticated endpoints:
  curl https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/pricing
  curl https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/reputation/8004/0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F
  curl https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/agent/0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F
  Note: agentId is the operator wallet address for ERC-8004 lookups.
  Record: HTTP status codes, response shapes, whether unauthenticated calls work.

STEP 2 — Register Invoica CEO Agent on Helixa:
  Our ERC-8004 identity was registered at Synthesis:
  - Registration tx: 0x3fd35faf69d7deb7a60b3b4032248bb3df1d1c599349ff801d12e104ceca9040 (Base Mainnet)
  - Operator wallet: 0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F
  Try: curl -X POST https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/agent/register \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"walletAddress": "0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F", "erc8004Tx": "0x3fd35faf69d7deb7a60b3b4032248bb3df1d1c599349ff801d12e104ceca9040"}'
  If endpoint shape differs, try GET https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/agent/0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F first.

STEP 3 — Obtain API key:
  Check Helixa docs/site for API key generation: https://helixa.xyz
  If self-serve: generate key, add to .env as HELIXA_API_KEY=<key>
  If requires outreach: DM @Bendr-20 on X/GitHub with:
    'Hey — we're Invoica (invoica.ai), x402 invoice middleware for AI agents on Base.
    We're integrating Helixa as our reputation oracle layer — using your Cred Score
    as 60% weight in our reputation API. Want to set up mutual integration?
    Our ERC-8004 identity: 0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F (Base Mainnet tx: 0x3fd35f...)'
  NOTE: Flag to Tarek if manual outreach is needed — he handles this.

STEP 4 — Add HELIXA_API_KEY to server .env:
  ssh invoica@65.108.90.178
  echo 'HELIXA_API_KEY=<key>' >> /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/.env
  Also add to local .env for development.

STEP 5 — Write report to reports/cto/helixa-001-api-setup-2026-03-20.md:
  - Unauthenticated endpoint results (status codes + response shapes)
  - Registration outcome
  - API key obtained: yes/no
  - If not: outreach drafted/sent, awaiting response
  - Response shape of /api/v2/agent/:id (document for REPUTATION-001 integration)

tasks.description: Build Helixa trust-gated invoice acceptance — check Cred Score before accepting incoming invoice payments from external agents
tasks.context:
ANTI-DESTRUCTIVE WRITE RULE — READ FIRST:
  invoices.ts is 1067 lines. After your change it MUST remain >=1000 lines.
  DO NOT rewrite, refactor, or restructure invoices.ts.
  Your ONLY changes = add 2 imports at top + ~10 lines inside POST /v1/invoices handler.

EXACT INSERTION POINT for invoices.ts:
  Line 3: import { checkTrustGate } from '../middleware/trust-gate';
  Line 594 (inside try block, BEFORE req.body destructuring):
    if (req.body.payerAgentId || req.body.agentId) {
      const gate = await checkTrustGate(payerAddr);
      if (gate.tier === 'rejected') { res.status(403).json({ error: 'Agent trust score too low' }); return; }
      (req as any)._trustGate = gate;
    }

CODE AGENT SCOPE — READ FIRST: write files ONLY, no installs/scripts/env changes

When an external agent submits a payment request to Invoica, we now check their Helixa Cred Score to determine acceptance policy. This addresses the oracle-of-ground-truth concern raised publicly — high-trust agents get streamlined settlement, low-trust agents get extra verification.

HELIXA API (confirmed 2026-03-20 — no auth needed for reads):
  IMPORTANT: /api/v2/agent/:id takes a NUMERIC tokenId, NOT a wallet address (wallet address causes overflow error).
  For external payer agents we only have wallet address → must use reputation endpoint.

  FOR EXTERNAL AGENTS (payers — wallet address only):
    PRIMARY: GET https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/reputation/8004/{walletAddress} → response.avgScore (0-100 or null)
    No fallback needed — this endpoint works for all ERC-8004 agents.

  FOR OUR OWN AGENT (Invoica CEO Agent — tokenId known):
    GET https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/agent/1072 → response.credScore (currently 31)
    agentAddress: 0x260E18591371A6E7A3da0AC5f9d47Ff06508B61F, soulbound: true, framework: openclaw

  If reputation endpoint returns null or 4xx → return null (treat as Marginal tier)
  No Authorization header needed. No HELIXA_API_KEY env var.
  Invoica CEO Agent minted 2026-03-20T17:35:19Z, tokenId: 1072, credScore: 31.

TRUST GATE LOGIC (confirmed tier boundaries from Helixa founder 2026-03-20):
  Helixa tiers: Junk=0-25, Marginal=26-50, Qualified=51-75, Prime=76-90, Preferred=91-100

  Score >= 51 (Qualified / Prime / Preferred): auto-accept, standard settlement flow
  Score 26-50 (Marginal): accept with hold — settlement confirmation requires 2x block confirmations
  Score 0-25 (Junk): reject invoice creation, return 403 with reason
  Score unknown/null (agent not on Helixa, API unavailable): accept with standard hold (treat as Marginal)

  NOTE: Invoica CEO Agent current score = 48 (Marginal tier, profile populated by Bendr 2026-03-20)

FILE: backend/src/lib/helixa.ts (NEW FILE — create from scratch, max 60 lines)
  Export: async function fetchHelixaScore(walletAddress: string): Promise<number | null>
  Implementation:
    IMPORTANT: /api/v2/agent/:id expects a numeric tokenId — passing a wallet address causes overflow.
    For external agents we only have wallet address, so always use the reputation endpoint.
    1. GET https://api.helixa.xyz/api/v2/reputation/8004/{walletAddress} → if 200 and response.avgScore != null, return response.avgScore
    2. If null or 4xx: return null
    3. Timeout: 5s (use AbortController)
    4. Log: [helixa] addr=<addr> score=<n|null>
  NOTE: Do NOT import from reputation.ts — this file is standalone.
  NOTE: REPUTATION-001 (week-101) will import from this file when it runs.

FILE: backend/src/middleware/trust-gate.ts (NEW FILE, max 80 lines)
  Import fetchHelixaScore from '../lib/helixa'
  Export: async function checkTrustGate(agentId: string): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; tier: 'auto' | 'hold' | 'rejected'; score: number | null; reason: string }>
  - Calls fetchHelixaScore(agentId)
  - Returns tier based on score thresholds above
  - Logs outcome: [trust-gate] agentId=<id> score=<n> tier=<tier>

INTEGRATION POINT:
  Find the invoice creation route (likely POST /v1/invoices in backend/src/routes/invoices.ts)
  Read invoices.ts first to understand current structure.
  Add trust gate BEFORE invoice is written to DB:
    const gate = await checkTrustGate(req.body.payerAgentId || req.body.agentId);
    if (gate.tier === 'rejected') return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Agent trust score too low', score: gate.score });
    // attach gate result to invoice record for settlement routing
  IMPORTANT: Only apply trust gate when payerAgentId is present in request body.
  If no agentId (human user invoices): skip trust gate entirely.

TESTS: backend/src/middleware/__tests__/trust-gate.test.ts
  - auto-tier-for-score-51-plus (Qualified threshold)
  - hold-tier-for-score-26-to-50 (Marginal)
  - rejected-tier-for-score-0-to-25 (Junk)
  - auto-tier-when-helixa-unavailable (graceful fallback — treat as Marginal)
  - invoice-creation-blocked-for-junk-agent (score=15)
  - invoice-creation-allowed-for-qualified-agent (score=55)

AFTER WRITING: npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v node_modules — 0 new errors.

tasks.description: Add Helixa CredScore badge to the invoice detail page

review.summary: Well-implemented task that correctly adds Helixa reputation score display with proper error handling and follows all specifications

issues.description: Missing JSDoc documentation on the component and interface
Nested fetch call could be extracted to a separate function for better readability

strengths: Correctly implements both required files without code fences
Preserves all existing functionality in page.tsx (58 lines vs required ≥52)
Proper TypeScript typing with strict interface definitions
Graceful error handling - reputation fetch failures are silently ignored as specified
Clean conditional rendering with proper null checks
Implements all tier colors exactly as specified
Proper component structure with clear separation of concerns
Badge component usage follows existing patterns
State management follows React best practices

tasks.description: Draft @invoica_ai Twitter thread announcing Helixa integration — trust-gated settlements, federated reputation
tasks.context:
CMO SCOPE — READ FIRST: You are a CONTENT WRITING agent. Write the tweet thread as plain text in your response only. Do NOT create any files. Do NOT output file paths.

Context: @JMasterHamster raised a sharp challenge on March 20 2026 about Invoica's settlement oracle problem. Helixa integration is the direct answer. CMO drafts the announcement thread.

THREAD STRUCTURE (3-4 tweets):

Tweet 1 — The problem framing:
  Reference @JMasterHamster's oracle question. Acknowledge it's the right question.
  'Who's the oracle of ground truth when your indexer fails?' Great question @JMasterHamster.
  Our answer: the oracle IS the chain. But trust starts before settlement.

Tweet 2 — The solution:
  Invoica is integrating @helixa_xyz as our reputation oracle.
  Helixa Cred Score (60%) + Invoica payment history (40%) = federated trust score.
  High-trust agents: auto-accept. Marginal: hold. Junk: rejected.
  1,071 agents scored in Phase 1. Cold-start solved.

Tweet 3 — The technical detail:
  Our /v1/reputation/:agentId endpoint now returns a federated score.
  Source breakdown visible in API response.
  Both use x402 + USDC on Base. Trust is now portable across the agent economy.

Tweet 4 (optional) — CTA:
  Invoica CEO Agent ERC-8004 identity: [basescan tx link]
  Register your agent. Get scored. Start earning.
  invoica.ai

RULES:
  - Only reference LIVE features (reputation endpoint will be live when REPUTATION-001 ships)
  - Do not post until REPUTATION-001 is deployed — queue this for after REPUTATION-001 merges
  - Each tweet max 280 chars
  - No emoji overuse — max 1-2 per tweet
  - Technically precise, not hype
